summary: Large symbol-labeling pass for sysdolphin/baselib and its many Melee callsites. The PR replaced anonymous func_8037xxxx-style names with HSD/AXDriver names in assembly and header declarations, including HSD_MemAlloc, HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress, HSD_ArchiveGetExtern, HSD_ArchiveLocateExtern, HSD_SList* routines, HSD_Perf* routines, HSD_ShadowStartRender, drawBackgroundRect, and several AXDriver functions. It also renamed two BSS allocation-data globals to slist_alloc_data and dlist_alloc_data. The author explicitly framed the work as correcting or filling in sysdolphin labels based on prior sysdolphin experience; there was no recorded review discussion.
what_changed: Renamed many branch targets from raw address-style func_* symbols to sysdolphin/HSD names across 61 files, with 382 added and 382 deleted lines, indicating a pure relabeling pass rather than behavior changes.

Replaced func_8037F1E4 callsites with HSD_MemAlloc throughout Melee and sysdolphin assembly, including examples in cm/code_80028B9C.s, db/code_80225374.s, grbigblue.s, grgreens.s, grhomerun.s, grstadium.s, sislib.s, and tobj.s.
Replaced func_80380358 callsites with HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress across fighter, stage, menu, item, and baselib code.
Labeled archive entry points in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/archive.s as HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress, HSD_ArchiveGetExtern, and HSD_ArchiveLocateExtern, and reflected those names in include/functions.h.
Labeled list-related sysdolphin routines in include/functions.h as HSD_SListAlloc, HSD_SListAllocAndAppend, HSD_SListPrepend, HSD_SListAppendList, HSD_SListPrependList, and HSD_SListRemove.
Labeled performance routines as HSD_PerfSetStartTime, HSD_PerfSetCPUTime, HSD_PerfSetDrawTime, HSD_PerfSetTotalTime, and HSD_PerfCountEnvelopeBlending.
Renamed shadow-related symbols including func_8037F7A0 to HSD_ShadowStartRender and func_8037F65C to drawBackgroundRect.
Renamed AXDriver symbols in headers and assembly, including AXDriverUnlink, AXDriverKeyOff, and AXDriverSetupAux.
Renamed BSS globals lbl_804C2580 and lbl_804C25AC to slist_alloc_data and dlist_alloc_data, and updated include/variables.h comments to match.
smart_moves: Updated both the defining sysdolphin assembly labels and all visible branch callsites, making the new names useful project-wide instead of only at the baselib definition site.
Kept the diff mechanically balanced as add/delete relabeling with no instruction-byte changes in the excerpt, reducing risk while improving readability.
Used established HSD_* and AXDriver* prefixes instead of ad hoc names, matching the library/module identity and making callsite roles immediately recognizable.
Renamed allocation-data globals from anonymous lbl_* symbols to purpose-oriented names, which helps future work connect HSD list routines to their BSS backing storage.
Targeted high-fanout primitives such as memory allocation and archive lookup; labeling these makes many unrelated Melee modules easier to understand even before those modules are decompiled.
decomp_lessons: High-confidence library routine names should be propagated through all assembly callsites and headers, not just the original symbol definition, because shared helpers like HSD_MemAlloc and HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress clarify many later decompilation targets.
When labeling SDK/library functions, preserve namespace-style prefixes such as HSD_, HSD_SList, HSD_Archive, HSD_Perf, and AXDriver to encode subsystem and expected API family.
BSS storage tied to allocator/list subsystems should be named after its role, such as slist_alloc_data and dlist_alloc_data, rather than left as address labels.
A pure relabeling PR can still materially improve decomp velocity: replacing func_* calls with library API names gives future C translations stronger semantic anchors.
The PR body says some labels were wrong or missing in other symbol maps, so symbol-map imports should be verified against local sysdolphin knowledge and callsite behavior rather than copied blindly.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_MemAlloc for the sysdolphin allocation routine previously labeled func_8037F1E4.
Use HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress for the archive public-symbol lookup routine previously labeled func_80380358.
Use HSD_ArchiveGetExtern and HSD_ArchiveLocateExtern for adjacent archive extern-resolution routines.
Use HSD_SList* names for singly-linked list helpers: HSD_SListAlloc, HSD_SListAllocAndAppend, HSD_SListPrepend, HSD_SListAppendList, HSD_SListPrependList, HSD_SListRemove.
Use HSD_Perf* names for performance-stat timing helpers: HSD_PerfSetStartTime, HSD_PerfSetCPUTime, HSD_PerfSetDrawTime, HSD_PerfSetTotalTime, HSD_PerfCountEnvelopeBlending.
Use AXDriver* names for audio driver helpers such as AXDriverUnlink, AXDriverKeyOff, and AXDriverSetupAux.
Use descriptive lowercase snake_case for internal allocation-data BSS objects such as slist_alloc_data and dlist_alloc_data.
Not every sysdolphin label necessarily receives an HSD_ prefix: drawBackgroundRect appears as a lower-camel descriptive helper near shadow rendering.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Assembly-only symbol relabeling can be done by replacing branch targets while preserving original instruction bytes and addresses, e.g. `bl func_8037F1E4` to `bl HSD_MemAlloc`.
For high-fanout functions, update callsites across unrelated systems in one pass so downstream decompilers see consistent API names everywhere.
Header comment declarations in include/functions.h should be renamed alongside assembly labels so symbol search and future prototype work stay aligned.
Variable declarations in include/variables.h should track BSS label renames to keep linker-visible labels and documentation comments synchronized.
Archive callsite context is a useful identification cue: many replacements of func_80380358 occur with archive pointer/string-name arguments and become HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that add prototypes or C implementations for HSD_MemAlloc and HSD_Free.
Find later PRs that decompile asm/sysdolphin/baselib/archive.s after HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress was labeled.
Find later PRs involving HSD_SListAlloc, HSD_SListPrepend, or slist_alloc_data.
Find later corrections to AXDriverUnlink, AXDriverKeyOff, or AXDriverSetupAux naming.
Search whether drawBackgroundRect kept that name or was later moved under an HSD_Shadow naming scheme.
